source_metadata.title_raw: Stephen C . Harrell - I-19-216 - January 9, 2020 - Tulare

content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
1102 Q Street• Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Stephen C. Harrell 
2024 East Foster Drive 
Tulare, CA 93274 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. 1-19-216 
Dear Mr. Harrell: 
January 9, 2020 
This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the Political Reform Act (the 
"Act"). 1 Please note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 
FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is 
not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for 
additional advice. Because your question is general in nature and not limited to specific 
governmental decisions, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2 
QUESTION 
Does the Act prohibit you, a member of the Tulare Local Health Care District Board of 
Directors and the Tulare County Independent Ambulance Compliance Committee, from 
additionally holding office as a City of Tulare city councilmember? 
CONCLUSION 
No. The Act does not prohibit an individual from seeking or holding multiple public offices. 
However, we encourage you to consult the attorneys for your current and prospective agencies 
about any other laws that may apply prior to becoming a candidate for the Tulare City Council. 
FACTS AS PRESENTED BY REQUESTER 
The Board of Supervisors for the County of Tulare has appointed you to both the Tulare 
Local Health Care District Board of Directors and the Tulare County Independent Ambulance 
Compliance Committee, and you currently serve on both of those bodies. A group of citizens have 
1 The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory 
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices 
Commission (the "Commission") are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of 
Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations unless otherwise 
indicated. 
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requester with the immunity provided by an opinion or formal 
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329( c )(3).) 


File No. 1-19-216 
Page No. 2 
asked you to consider running for a seat on the Tulare City Council in 2020. You have asked 
whether the Act would prohibit you from additionally holding that office. 
ANALYSIS 
The Act does not prohibit an individual from seeking or holding multiple public offices.3 
Laws outside the Commission's jurisdiction, however, may restrict an individual from holding two 
public offices simultaneously. For instance, an individual may be prohibited from holding two or 
more public offices simultaneously if those offices are determined to be "incompatible offices" (see 
Section 1099), and a local agency officer or employee may be prohibited from engaging in an 
activity that his or her local agency has determined incompatible with the officer or employee's 
duties (see Section 1126). Therefore, we encourage you to consult the attorneys for your current and 
prospective agencies about any other laws that may apply prior to becoming a candidate for the 
Tulare City Council. 
If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660. 
MFC:aja 
Sincerely, 
Dave Bainbridge 
General Counsel 
By: 
Matthew F. Christy 
Counsel, Legal Division 
3 The Act's conflict of interest provisions prohibit a public official from taking part in a governmental decision 
ifit is reasonably foreseeable that the decision would have a material financial effect on one or more of the official's 
financial interests distinguishable from the decision's effect on the public generally. (Sections 87100 and 87103.) Those 
provisions apply on a decision by decision basis, and the outcome of their application is based on the specific facts 
relating to the decision at issue. Because you have not identified a specific governmental decision at issue, the Act's 
conflict of interest provisions do not apply.

CONCLUSION: No. The Act does not prohibit an individual from seeking or holding multiple public offices.
